summary: Iteration 3 — TerrainPRO 7-day free-trial auth (JWT email/password). Backend: 9/9 pytest pass for /api/auth/register (token + trial_active=true + days_remaining=7 + trial_expires_at ~7d ahead), duplicate email -> 409, short password -> 422, /api/auth/login (demo demo@terrainpro.com/trade1234 success, wrong password -> 401, unknown email -> 401), /api/auth/me (missing token -> 401, invalid token -> 401, valid token returns user with live trial fields). Frontend Playwright on public URL verifies: /signup renders with high-contrast yellow trial badge '[ No Credit Card Required — 7 Days Free Access ]'; signup with valid form auto-logs in and lands on /dashboard with banner '[ System Status: Trial Active // 7 Days Remaining ]' and the EstimatorWizard workspace; workspace-signout clears session and returns home, then /dashboard direct hit redirects to /signup; demo user login lands on /dashboard; hero-primary-cta and menu-start-trial both route to /signup. All 9 frontend scenarios PASS.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: JWT secret loaded via os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] inside _jwt_secret() each call — fine, but if env var is missing the first auth request 500s with KeyError instead of a clear startup failure. Validate at startup.

server.py /api/auth/register: when an 'existing' user has no password_hash (i.e. a /api/signup row using the older free-trial gate model), the new register path overwrites their entire doc via $set (including created_at). That's intentional but mixes two account systems on the same `users` collection — consider documenting or migrating /api/signup rows to a separate collection.

server.py: TOKEN_TTL_DAYS=7 == TRIAL_DAYS=7, so when the trial ends the JWT also expires. Functional today, but if trial length ever changes (e.g. paid users get a new trial_expires_at), token TTL must be decoupled.
server.py _public_user: days_remaining uses math.ceil so a user 0.1s into trial reads '7'; matches the spec ('~7 days ahead'), but consider exposing seconds_remaining for precise UI countdowns.

AuthContext.jsx: setting axios.defaults.headers globally means any other axios usage in the app inherits the Bearer token; this is fine for a single-tenant SPA but be aware if you ever add 3rd-party axios calls.

AuthContext.jsx: fetchMe failure silently logs the user out (applyToken(null) + setUser(false)). On a transient network blip the user is bounced to /signup. Consider distinguishing 401 from network/5xx and retrying once.
test_credentials: Demo trial user: demo@terrainpro.com / trade1234 (works). New registrations use random qa_<uuid>@test.com.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run inserts 3-4 new users via /api/auth/register (TEST_ prefix not enforced — qa_<uuid>@test.com). One UI signup test also creates a user via the frontend. All under db.users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend auth tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_auth.py (run: pytest backend/tests/test_auth.py). Public URL: https://pro-quote-builder-1.preview.emergentagent.com. Auth = Bearer token in Authorization header (NOT cookies). Token stored in localStorage 'terrainpro:token'. data-testids confirmed working: signup-page, signup-trial-badge, signup-name/phone/email/password, signup-submit, login-page, login-email/password, login-submit, trial-banner, workspace-page, workspace-signout, hero-primary-cta, menu-start-trial.
rca of the issue: n/a — no issues found. Implementation matches spec end-to-end.
